topic: Aspects of Political Life in the French-speaking World
subject: french

items.q: In which year did Algeria gain independence from France?
items.why: Students confuse the start of French colonisation of Algeria (1830) with independence (1962), reached after the Algerian War (1954–62).

items.q: What is 'la Francophonie' as a political organisation?
items.correct: An international body promoting cooperation among French-speaking states
items.why: La Francophonie (OIF) links many countries where French is spoken; students wrongly think it means only France itself.

items.q: What does decolonisation refer to in francophone political history?
items.correct: The process by which former colonies gained independence from France
items.random_wrong: The founding of the Fifth Republic
items.why: Decolonisation concerns colonies becoming independent; students confuse it with unrelated French domestic political events.

items.q: Which is NOT part of the Maghreb?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Sénégal. “Algérie” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: In what year was Quebec's Law 101 (Charter of the French Language) passed?
items.why: Exact answer: 1977. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
